Michigan · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Board of Speech-Language Pathology under the Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$118
$68 application + $50 license fee
Renewal
$118
Every 2 years
Processing Time
4 to 6 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Board of Speech-Language Pathology

Mailing Address

P.O. Box 30670, Lansing, MI 48909-8170

Physical Address

611 W. Ottawa St., Lansing, MI 48933

Phone & Fax

Phone: (517) 335-0918

Fax: (517) 373-2179

Email

bplhelp@michigan.gov

Credential Categories

Michigan SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Michigan.

Temporary SLP License

Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Michigan Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on Praxis (test code 5331, score 162).

Complete Clinical Fellowship

Complete 36 weeks of supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

Submit Application to LARA

Submit application with fees through LARA online system.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF completion documentation.
  • Background checkCriminal background check.
Telepractice

Michigan Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Michigan permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. Michigan is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. A valid Michigan SLP license is required.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Michigan issues a Temporary SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Michigan does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Michigan licensure.

Frequently Asked Questions

Michigan SLP FAQ

How much does a Michigan SLP license cost?

$118 initial. $118 biennial renewal.

Is Michigan part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

No. Michigan has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

Does Michigan allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes, with a valid Michigan license.
